info.title: Pilot Status API
info.description: Public REST API for Pilot Status. Authenticate with the `x-api-key: ps_...` header (or `x-api-key-id`). Base URL: https://pilotstatus.com.br

get.description: Lists every WhatsApp number on the tenant (PILOT_STATUS and Meta Cloud API), including connection state and API key prefixes. Authenticate with x-api-key-id (or x-api-key: ps_…).

post.description: Creates an unofficial WhatsApp number (PILOT_STATUS) and returns the QR code + pairing code to pair it. By default links the number to the calling API key (set linkToApiKey:false to skip). Real side effect: provisions an instance.

linkToApiKey.description: Link the new number to the calling API key (default true).

post.description: Manual Meta Cloud API onboarding: you already own the WABA + system-user token and pass the credentials directly. Returns the created number + the webhook URL/verifyToken you must configure in your Meta app. (No credentials yet? Use /api/v1/numbers/remote-pairing with provider=META.)

metaPhoneId.description: Meta Cloud API Phone Number ID.

linkToApiKey.description: Link the number to the calling API key (default true).

post.description: Generates a link so another person connects their own number. provider=PILOT_STATUS (default) requires name + number and returns a QR pairing link; provider=META returns a hosted Embedded Signup link (the person picks the number inside the Facebook dialog, no name/number).

name.description: Required for PILOT_STATUS; ignored for META.

number.description: Required for PILOT_STATUS; ignored for META.

sendViaWhatsApp.description: Send the link over WhatsApp (PILOT_STATUS). Default false.

linkToApiKey.description: Link the resulting number to the calling API key (default true).

get.description: Returns the number + all Meta info when it is an official-API number: a nested `meta` block (phone / profile / waba, camelCase) with quality, messaging-limit tier, verification status, business profile and WABA data, plus `health`. Served from cache by default; pass `?refresh=1` to fetch live from the Meta Graph and persist. Non-Meta numbers return `meta: null`. (The same route accepts DELETE to remove; use GET /api/v1/numbers/:id/status for a lightweight state-only poll.)

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: WhatsApp number id.

parameters.name: refresh
parameters.description: Pass `1` to force a live fetch from the Meta Graph and refresh the cache.

delete.description: Permanently removes the WhatsApp number/instance, cleaning up upstream provider resources. Real and irreversible side effect.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Id of the number/instance to remove.

get.description: Syncs and returns the number's current connection state (OPEN/CONNECTING/…). Meta numbers return OPEN. Useful to poll after creating/connecting.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Number/instance id.

get.description: Generates a fresh QR code + pairing code to reconnect a disconnected PILOT_STATUS number. Returns 409 if already OPEN. Not applicable to Meta numbers.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: PILOT_STATUS number/instance id.

get.summary: Preview buying an extra number (On Demand)
get.description:
Computes the cost of buying N extra number(s) without charging. Requires a TENANT-scoped key (403 for a number key). For Free-plan tenants it flags the On Demand conversion: the free number becomes paid/unlimited and the new one too (you pay per number/mo). On Demand = unlimited messages on every number.

**Requires a tenant-scoped key.**

parameters.name: quantity
parameters.description: How many numbers to buy (default 1).

200.description: Preview — Free tenant with 1 connected number buying 1 (converts to On Demand). `proratedTotal` is charged now; `monthlyTotalBRL` recurs each cycle.

explanation.pt: Sua conta passa para o plano On Demand: mensagens ilimitadas e R$ 29,90/mês por número conectado. No On Demand todo número é pago — o número que você já usa deixa de ser gratuito. Com o novo, são 2 números a R$ 29,90: R$ 59,80/mês.
explanation.en: Your account moves to the On Demand plan: unlimited messages and R$ 29.90/month per connected number. On On Demand every number is paid — the number you already use stops being free. With the new one, that is 2 numbers at R$ 29.90: R$ 59.80/month.

403.description: Number-scoped key used on a tenant-only endpoint

example.error: Number-scoped keys cannot call tenant endpoints

post.description:
Buys N extra number(s). Requires a TENANT-scoped key. A Free tenant is converted to On Demand (bills the converted free number + the new one) — requires confirm:true, otherwise returns 409 CONFIRMATION_REQUIRED with the preview. With a saved card (Plans → Cartão) it is charged immediately; otherwise it returns { checkoutUrl } to pay. Real side effect: it charges. Stripe only.

**Requires a tenant-scoped key.**

confirm.description: Required (true) when the purchase converts the plan (Free → On Demand).

200.description: Bought 1 (with confirmation). The prorated cost was funded from wallet credits and/or the saved card.

403.description: Number-scoped key used on a tenant-only endpoint

example.error: Number-scoped keys cannot call tenant endpoints

409.description: The purchase converts the Free plan to On Demand, which changes what you pay — resend with `confirm: true`. The full preview is included.

example.error: Confirmation required: buying an extra number converts the Free plan to On Demand (every number becomes billable). Resend with confirm:true.

explanation.pt: Sua conta passa para o plano On Demand: mensagens ilimitadas e R$ 29,90/mês por número conectado. No On Demand todo número é pago — o número que você já usa deixa de ser gratuito. Com o novo, são 2 números a R$ 29,90: R$ 59,80/mês.
explanation.en: Your account moves to the On Demand plan: unlimited messages and R$ 29.90/month per connected number. On On Demand every number is paid — the number you already use stops being free. With the new one, that is 2 numbers at R$ 29.90: R$ 59.80/month.

delete.description:
Gives back N paid extra-number slot(s) — the inverse of POST. Requires a TENANT-scoped key. NO refund for the current cycle; it only stops future billing. Deleting a number (DELETE /v1/numbers/{id}) already releases its slot automatically. Returns 409 NUMBER_LIMIT_EXCEEDED if the reduction would drop the limit below currently connected numbers.

**Requires a tenant-scoped key.**

quantity.description: How many extra number slots to release (default 1).

403.description: Number-scoped key used on a tenant-only endpoint

example.error: Number-scoped keys cannot call tenant endpoints

409.description: Reduction would drop the limit below connected numbers

example.error: Você tem 2 número(s) conectado(s); remover 2 deixaria o limite em 0. Desconecte números antes de remover slots pagos.

post.description:
Creates a hosted Stripe checkout page and returns the url for the user to open in a browser — nothing is charged until they complete it there. purpose:"wallet_topup" (requires amount > 0, in the account currency) mints a one-time payment that credits the pre-paid wallet; purpose:"add_card" mints a page to save a card on file. Account-level action: requires a TENANT-scoped key (403 NUMBER_SCOPE_NOT_ALLOWED for a number key). Stripe only.

**Requires a tenant-scoped key.**

purpose.description: What the checkout page is for.

amount.description: Amount to credit to the wallet, in the account currency (required for wallet_topup).

403.description: Number-scoped key used on a tenant-only endpoint

example.error: Number-scoped keys cannot call tenant endpoints

post.description:
Enqueues sending a message from the number linked to the API key. Use exactly one of templateId (template send, with variables) or text (free-form); and exactly one destination: destinationNumber (E.164), groupId (…@g.us) or newsletterId (…@newsletter). Requires a number-scoped key (403 for a tenant key). Real side effect: actually delivers and may cost. NOT_SUPPORTED_FOR_META for groupId/newsletterId on Meta numbers.

**Requires a number-scoped key.**

templateId.description: Template id or name (mutually exclusive with text).

destinationNumber.description: Destination (exactly one). Accepts an E.164 phone OR a BSUID — the contact's durable userId (e.g. BR.13491208655302741918), returned by GET /api/v1/conversations. Use the BSUID to reply to contacts using a WhatsApp username (no phone). One-tap/zero-tap/copy-code authentication templates require a phone number.

media.description: Public http(s) URL OR base64 data URI (e.g. data:audio/ogg;base64,AAAA…). Base64 works on Meta Cloud API and Evolution v2; on Evolution GO use a public http(s) URL (GO does not accept base64). Used in template mode or in direct-media mode (media + mediaType, without templateId and without text).

mediaType.description: Media type. In direct-media mode (no templateId/text) send media + mediaType: optional caption for image/video/document (not for audio); buttons/header/footer/variables not allowed. "audio" is delivered as a voice note (PTT) on Meta Cloud API, Evolution v2, and Evolution GO; on Evolution v2 and GO a "recording audio" presence indicator is shown to the recipient before the voice note (Meta has no outbound presence API).

mediaFilename.description: Filename the document arrives with in WhatsApp. Direct document sends only (requires mediaType: document). When omitted the name falls back to the URL basename. Path separators are stripped. Rejected with 400 when sent without media, on a template send, or with any mediaType other than document.

buttons.description:
Up to **3** interactive buttons. Each item has `type` + `displayText` plus one type-specific field.

- `reply` — quick-reply button; works on **free-form** and **template** sends. Set `id` (your own value; **not** echoed back on tap — match replies by label + `quotedMessageId`).
- `url` — opens a link; set `url` (https). On free-form sends **at most one URL button per message**, and it **may** be combined with `reply` buttons.
- `call` — dials a number; set `phoneNumber` (E.164, e.g. `+5511999999999`). **Template sends only** — rejected with 400 (`BUTTON_PHONE_NUMBER_TEMPLATE_ONLY`) on free-form.
- `copy` — copies a code; set `copyCode`. **Template sends only** — rejected with 400 (`BUTTON_COPY_CODE_TEMPLATE_ONLY`) on free-form.

On a **template** send, buttons override the template's own buttons.

id.description: `reply` only — identifier you assign (not echoed back on tap).

header.description: Header for a free-form interactive message. Requires `buttons`. `type` is `text` (up to 60 chars in `content`) or `image` / `video` / `document` (public URL in `content`).

content.description: Text (type=text, ≤60 chars) or a public media URL (image/video/document).

deliverAt.description: Schedule delivery for a future time.

labels.description: Labels to apply to the destination conversation.

403.description: Tenant-scoped key used on a number-scoped endpoint

example.error: Tenant-scoped keys cannot call number endpoints

404.description: Template not found or without an approved version

get.description:
Returns a message's status and timestamps. The {id} can be the internal message id, the correlationId, or the provider message id (externalMessageId / Meta wamid). Includes externalMessageId, status, destination, sentAt/deliveredAt/readAt and error if any. Requires a number-scoped key.

**Requires a number-scoped key.**

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Internal id, correlationId, or externalMessageId (Meta wamid).

403.description: Tenant-scoped key used on a number-scoped endpoint

example.error: Tenant-scoped keys cannot call number endpoints

delete.description:
Cancels SCHEDULED (not yet sent) messages by an array of messageIds (1–100). Requires a number-scoped key. Real side effect on the send queue.

**Requires a number-scoped key.**

messageIds.description: Ids of scheduled messages to cancel.

403.description: Tenant-scoped key used on a number-scoped endpoint

example.error: Tenant-scoped keys cannot call number endpoints

get.description:
Lists the linked number's conversations, ordered by last activity (lastMessageAt desc). Paginated via page/pageSize. Filter by date with startDate/endDate (ISO 8601, over lastMessageAt). The number's privacy (PII) mode affects the response: RELAY_ONLY returns [] + notice:"PII_RELAY_ONLY"; STORE_X_DAYS clamps to the window max(startDate, now − retentionDays). Requires a number-scoped key. Contact identity: `from` is the E.164 phone (with `+`) when known — it may be null for contacts using a WhatsApp username; `userId` is the durable BSUID (stable contact id) and `username` the public handle when visible.

**Requires a number-scoped key.**

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Start of the date range (filters by lastMessageAt). Must be ≤ endDate.

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: End of the date range (filters by lastMessageAt).

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page (default 1).

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Items per page (default 30, max 100).

example.error: startDate must be before or equal to endDate

403.description: Tenant-scoped key used on a number-scoped endpoint

example.error: Tenant-scoped keys cannot call number endpoints

get.description:
Lists messages in the linked number's GROUP conversations (newest first). Paginated via page/pageSize. Filter by date with startDate/endDate (ISO 8601, over createdAt). Meta numbers return 400 NOT_SUPPORTED_FOR_META (groups are PILOT_STATUS-only). The number's privacy (PII) mode applies: RELAY_ONLY → [] + notice:"PII_RELAY_ONLY"; STORE_X_DAYS clamps to the window max(startDate, now − retentionDays). Requires a number-scoped key.

**Requires a number-scoped key.**

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Start of the date range (filters by createdAt). Must be ≤ endDate.

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: End of the date range (filters by createdAt).

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page (default 1).

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Items per page (default 30, max 100).

example.error: Group messages are not supported for Meta Cloud API numbers

403.description: Tenant-scoped key used on a number-scoped endpoint

example.error: Tenant-scoped keys cannot call number endpoints

get.description:
Lists the linked number's full message history (direct and group), ordered by providerTimestamp (newest first). Works for all providers (Evolution Go, Evolution v2 and Meta). Paginated via page/pageSize. Filter by date with startDate/endDate (ISO 8601, over providerTimestamp). Returns up to ~1 month of stored history (from the connection date). The number's privacy (PII) mode applies: RELAY_ONLY → [] + notice:"PII_RELAY_ONLY"; STORE_X_DAYS clamps to the window max(startDate, now − retentionDays). Requires a number-scoped key.

**Requires a number-scoped key.**

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Start of the date range (filters by providerTimestamp). Must be ≤ endDate.

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: End of the date range (filters by providerTimestamp).

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page (default 1).

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Items per page (default 30, max 100).

403.description: Tenant-scoped key used on a number-scoped endpoint

example.error: Tenant-scoped keys cannot call number endpoints

get.description:
Lists the linked number's unread INBOUND messages (newest first). "total" is the TRUE unread count — the sum of Conversation.unreadCount (the same counter the dashboard shows), not a raw count of inbound messages. Each item carries conversationUnreadCount (the conversation's unread total). Paginated via page/pageSize (no date range). The number's privacy (PII) mode applies: RELAY_ONLY → [] + notice:"PII_RELAY_ONLY"; STORE_X_DAYS clamps to the retention window. Requires a number-scoped key.

**Requires a number-scoped key.**

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page (default 1).

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Items per page (default 30, max 100).

403.description: Tenant-scoped key used on a number-scoped endpoint

example.error: Tenant-scoped keys cannot call number endpoints

get.description:
Reports whether the WhatsApp 24-hour customer-service window is currently OPEN for a contact on the number bound to the API key.

- **Meta Cloud API numbers** — `windowType: "META_24H"`. `open` is true when the contact's last inbound message arrived within the last 24 hours; `expiresAt` is `lastInboundAt` + 24h. While the window is open, free-form messages are allowed; once it closes, sends fail with `META_OUTSIDE_24H_WINDOW` and you must use an approved template.
- **Web (unofficial) numbers** — no Meta restriction: `windowType: "NONE"` and `open` is always true; `lastInboundAt`/`expiresAt` are still returned informatively.

Check it before sending free-form content to decide between free-form and template messages.

**Requires a number-scoped key.**

parameters.name: destinationNumber
parameters.description: Contact phone number in E.164 format, with or without the leading + (digits also accepted).

open.description: Whether free-form messages can be sent right now. Always true for web (unofficial) numbers.

windowType.description: META_24H for Meta Cloud API numbers (24-hour window enforced); NONE for web (unofficial) numbers.

lastInboundAt.description: UTC ISO timestamp of the contact's most recent inbound message, or null when none is known.

expiresAt.description: UTC ISO timestamp when the window closes (lastInboundAt + 24h), or null. Informative on web numbers.

example.error: destinationNumber is required and must be a valid phone (E.164, with or without +)

403.description: Tenant-scoped key used on a number-scoped endpoint

example.error: Tenant-scoped keys cannot call number endpoints

get.description:
Lists WhatsApp groups for the linked number (same instance as POST /api/v1/messages/send). Requires an OPEN instance (409 WHATSAPP_INSTANCE_NOT_CONNECTED if disconnected). With no linked instance returns { groups: [] }. NOT_SUPPORTED_FOR_META: Meta numbers return 400 NOT_SUPPORTED_FOR_META. Requires a number-scoped key.

**Requires a number-scoped key.**

example.error: Groups are not supported for Meta Cloud API numbers

403.description: Tenant-scoped key used on a number-scoped endpoint

example.error: Tenant-scoped keys cannot call number endpoints

get.description:
Lists the linked number's channels/newsletters. Same instance resolution, OPEN requirement and 409 WHATSAPP_INSTANCE_NOT_CONNECTED as GET /api/v1/groups. With no linked instance returns { newsletters: [] }. NOT_SUPPORTED_FOR_META: Meta numbers return 400 NOT_SUPPORTED_FOR_META. Requires a number-scoped key.

**Requires a number-scoped key.**

example.error: Newsletters are not supported for Meta Cloud API numbers

403.description: Tenant-scoped key used on a number-scoped endpoint

example.error: Tenant-scoped keys cannot call number endpoints

get.description:
Lists templates available for the linked number (scoped by number; WABA-shared for Meta). Each item has id, name, category, body, variables, plus source ("META" | "PILOT_STATUS"), metaStatus ("APPROVED" | "PENDING" | "REJECTED" | "DISABLED" | null), metaLanguage and sendable (boolean). On a Meta Cloud API number only source=META + metaStatus=APPROVED templates are sendable; non-Meta templates are not sendable on a Meta number. Requires a number-scoped key.

**Requires a number-scoped key.**

403.description: Tenant-scoped key used on a number-scoped endpoint

example.error: Tenant-scoped keys cannot call number endpoints

post.description:
Creates a template in the linked number's scope. The body field accepts three shapes: (1) a structured OBJECT { header?, body, footer?, buttons? } — recommended; (2) that same JSON as a STRING; or (3) PLAIN TEXT, auto-wrapped into { "body": { "text": ... } }. Structure: optional header NONE (default) | TEXT (0–1 variable, up to 60 chars) | IMAGE | VIDEO | DOCUMENT — media supplied via header.url (http/https) OR header.base64 (data URI data:<mime>;base64,…, re-hosted server-side); body.text up to 1024 chars, with named-style variables {{nome}} (must not start or end with a variable); footer.text up to 60 chars, no variables; buttons (up to 10 total) of types QUICK_REPLY (≤ 10), URL (≤ 2; static or dynamic with a trailing {{1}} in the URL + a resolved example), PHONE_NUMBER (≤ 1) and COPY_CODE (≤ 1; the code goes in button.example) — mixing quick-reply with CTA buttons is allowed. Buttons may accompany a video or document (PDF) header. The examples field is REQUIRED whenever the body/header (or the dynamic URL) uses variables: an object mapping each variable to a sample value; missing → 400 { code: "TEMPLATE_EXAMPLES_REQUIRED", details: { missing: [...] } }. For Meta numbers the template is submitted to Meta for approval (metaStatus PENDING); 400 META_TEMPLATE_SUBMIT_FAILED if Meta rejects it. 409 if a template with that name already exists. Requires a number-scoped key.

**Requires a number-scoped key.**

body.description: Template content: object { header?, body, footer?, buttons? } (recommended), the same JSON as a string, or plain text (auto-wrapped).

body.header.description: Optional header. TEXT: text with 0–1 variable (up to 60 chars). IMAGE/VIDEO/DOCUMENT: provide url (http/https) OR base64 (data URI); base64 is re-hosted server-side. No location header.

body.footer.description: Optional footer, up to 60 chars, no variables.

body.buttons.description: Up to 10 buttons: QUICK_REPLY (≤ 10; text), URL (≤ 2; text + static url or dynamic with trailing {{1}}), PHONE_NUMBER (≤ 1; text + phone_number E.164), COPY_CODE (≤ 1; code in example). Quick-reply and CTA can coexist.

url.description: `URL` only — static link, or dynamic with a trailing `{{1}}` variable.

example.description: `COPY_CODE` only — sample code(s) for the copyable value.

examples.description: Required when variables exist: maps each variable used in body/header (and the dynamic URL) to a sample value. The COPY_CODE code goes in button.example, not here. Missing → 400 TEMPLATE_EXAMPLES_REQUIRED.

body.text: Oi {{nome}}! Use e ganhe {{desconto}} de desconto.

403.description: Tenant-scoped key used on a number-scoped endpoint

example.error: Tenant-scoped keys cannot call number endpoints

put.description:
Updates an existing template (by id or name) in the linked number's scope. The body follows the same contract as Create template: body as an OBJECT { header?, body, footer?, buttons? } (recommended), the same JSON as a STRING, or PLAIN TEXT; header NONE | TEXT (0–1 variable, up to 60) | IMAGE | VIDEO | DOCUMENT (media via header.url OR header.base64, re-hosted); body.text up to 1024 (variables {{nome}}, must not start/end with a variable); footer up to 60 with no variables; up to 10 buttons — QUICK_REPLY (≤ 10), URL (≤ 2; static or dynamic with {{1}} + example), PHONE_NUMBER (≤ 1), COPY_CODE (≤ 1; code in button.example). The examples field is REQUIRED whenever variables exist; missing → 400 { code: "TEMPLATE_EXAMPLES_REQUIRED", details: { missing: [...] } }. For Meta numbers the new version is re-submitted to Meta for approval (metaStatus PENDING); 400 META_TEMPLATE_SUBMIT_FAILED if Meta rejects it. 404 TEMPLATE_NOT_FOUND if it doesn't exist in the number's scope. Requires a number-scoped key.

**Requires a number-scoped key.**

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Id or name of the template to update.

body.description: New template content: object { header?, body, footer?, buttons? } (recommended), the same JSON as a string, or plain text (auto-wrapped).

body.header.description: Optional header. TEXT: text with 0–1 variable (up to 60 chars). IMAGE/VIDEO/DOCUMENT: provide url (http/https) OR base64 (data URI); base64 is re-hosted server-side. No location header.

body.footer.description: Optional footer, up to 60 chars, no variables.

body.buttons.description: Up to 10 buttons: QUICK_REPLY (≤ 10; text), URL (≤ 2; text + static url or dynamic with trailing {{1}}), PHONE_NUMBER (≤ 1; text + phone_number E.164), COPY_CODE (≤ 1; code in example). Quick-reply and CTA can coexist.

url.description: `URL` only — static link, or dynamic with a trailing `{{1}}` variable.

example.description: `COPY_CODE` only — sample code(s) for the copyable value.

examples.description: Required when variables exist: maps each variable used in body/header (and the dynamic URL) to a sample value. The COPY_CODE code goes in button.example, not here. Missing → 400 TEMPLATE_EXAMPLES_REQUIRED.

403.description: Tenant-scoped key used on a number-scoped endpoint

example.error: Tenant-scoped keys cannot call number endpoints

get.description:
Returns a template by id (or name), with body and variables. Also includes source ("META" | "PILOT_STATUS"), metaStatus ("APPROVED" | "PENDING" | "REJECTED" | "DISABLED" | null), metaLanguage and sendable — on a Meta Cloud API number only source=META + metaStatus=APPROVED templates are sendable. 404 TEMPLATE_NOT_FOUND if it doesn't exist in the number's scope. Requires a number-scoped key.

**Requires a number-scoped key.**

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Template id or name.

403.description: Tenant-scoped key used on a number-scoped endpoint

example.error: Tenant-scoped keys cannot call number endpoints

delete.description:
Removes the template by id (or name) in the linked number's scope. For Meta templates it also removes the template from Meta (WABA). 404 TEMPLATE_NOT_FOUND if it doesn't exist or isn't owned by the number. Returns { deleted: true, id }. Requires a number-scoped key. Real and irreversible side effect.

**Requires a number-scoped key.**

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Id or name of the template to remove.

403.description: Tenant-scoped key used on a number-scoped endpoint

example.error: Tenant-scoped keys cannot call number endpoints

get.description:
Downloads a Meta Cloud API media by mediaId (e.g. from an inbound webhook) and returns it as a base64 data URI + mimeType. Requires the phoneNumberId query param (a tenant Meta number's metaPhoneId). Requires a number-scoped key.

**Requires a number-scoped key.**

parameters.name: mediaId
parameters.description: Meta Cloud API media id.

parameters.name: phoneNumberId
parameters.description: metaPhoneId of a tenant Meta number.

403.description: Tenant-scoped key used on a number-scoped endpoint

example.error: Tenant-scoped keys cannot call number endpoints

get.description:
Returns aggregated send metrics for the linked number (chart-ready series). Accepts period (7d|30d|90d, default 30d) and timezone (or tz, default UTC). 409 if the key is not linked to a number. Requires a number-scoped key.

**Requires a number-scoped key.**

parameters.name: period
parameters.description: Time window (default 30d).

parameters.name: timezone
parameters.description: Timezone (alias: tz; default UTC).

403.description: Tenant-scoped key used on a number-scoped endpoint

example.error: Tenant-scoped keys cannot call number endpoints

get.description:
Tenant-scoped only (a number-scoped key gets 403 NUMBER_SCOPE_NOT_ALLOWED, and an OAuth grant must be full-tenant). Returns a lean flat list — one item per tenant number — with numberId, number, displayName, keyId, keyLast4 and the real usable value (`key` field) when the key was created with encryption enabled (`revealable: true`). Legacy keys come back with `key: null` and `revealable: false` (regenerate that number to obtain a usable value).

**Requires a tenant-scoped key.**

403.description: Number-scoped key used on a tenant-only endpoint

example.error: Number-scoped keys cannot call tenant endpoints

post.description:
REGENERATES the default API key of a single number (the product keeps exactly one default key per number — there is no "create another key"). Tenant-scoped only (403 NUMBER_SCOPE_NOT_ALLOWED for a number key). Pass the number in whatsappNumberId: its previous key is rotated/invalidated and the new raw value (`key` field) is returned ONLY ONCE — store it. Unknown number → 404 NUMBER_NOT_FOUND.

**Requires a tenant-scoped key.**

whatsappNumberId.description: Id of the number whose default key will be regenerated.

403.description: Number-scoped key used on a tenant-only endpoint

example.error: Number-scoped keys cannot call tenant endpoints

post.description: Rotates THE API key for the calling scope — the product keeps ONE key per scope, not many. A number-scoped key rotates that number's default key; a tenant-scoped key rotates the tenant key. The old key is revoked and the new one (key field) is returned ONLY ONCE. Not available for per-number OAuth grants (403).

get.description: Lists the webhooks visible to the calling key. A tenant key sees ALL of the tenant's webhooks; with number context (a number-scoped key or the x-whatsapp-number-id header) it sees STRICTLY the webhooks subscribed to that number (tenant-wide webhooks with no subscription are NOT returned). The signing secret is NEVER returned. A per-number OAuth grant must send x-whatsapp-number-id (otherwise 400 NUMBER_REQUIRED).

parameters.name: x-whatsapp-number-id
parameters.description: Scopes to a single number (required for per-number OAuth grants).

post.description: Creates a webhook subscribed to ONE WhatsApp number. The number comes from the key scope (number key), the x-whatsapp-number-id header or the whatsappNumberId body field — if the body contradicts the key scope → 400 NUMBER_MISMATCH; no resolvable number → 400 NUMBER_REQUIRED; another tenant's number → 404 NUMBER_NOT_FOUND. events picks what gets delivered: an empty/omitted list dispatches NOTHING (there is no implicit "subscribe to all"; use "*" for all) and events the number's provider cannot emit are silently dropped. The signing secret is NEVER returned.

parameters.name: x-whatsapp-number-id
parameters.description: Scopes to a single number (required for per-number OAuth grants).

url.description: HTTPS endpoint that will receive the events.

events.description: Events to subscribe to (e.g. ["message.received"]); "*" = all. Empty/omitted = dispatches nothing.

whatsappNumberId.description: Target number — only needed with a tenant key and no number header; with a number key it must match the scope (400 NUMBER_MISMATCH).

get.description: Returns ONE webhook by id (read-only). 404 WEBHOOK_NOT_FOUND if the webhook isn't visible to the calling key — same number scope as GET /api/v1/webhooks. The signing secret is NEVER returned.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Webhook id.

parameters.name: x-whatsapp-number-id
parameters.description: Scopes to a single number (required for per-number OAuth grants).

patch.description: Updates a webhook: any subset of name, url, active (false pauses deliveries without deleting) and events. events REPLACES the full subscription list — empty list = dispatches nothing; "*" = all; events the number's provider cannot emit are dropped. The webhook's number CANNOT be changed via the public API (400 NUMBER_RESCOPE_NOT_SUPPORTED) — delete and recreate. 404 if not visible to the key. The secret is NEVER returned.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Webhook id.

parameters.name: x-whatsapp-number-id
parameters.description: Scopes to a single number (required for per-number OAuth grants).

events.description: Full REPLACEMENT list of subscriptions; "*" = all.

delete.description: PERMANENTLY deletes a webhook (irreversible): the subscription and its delivery logs are removed and the endpoint simply stops receiving events. To pause without losing the webhook, prefer PATCH with active=false. 404 if not visible to the calling key (same number scope as the list).

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Webhook id.

parameters.name: x-whatsapp-number-id
parameters.description: Scopes to a single number (required for per-number OAuth grants).

get.description: Lists a webhook's recent delivery events (read-only). Each item carries event, status (SUCCESS|FAILED), statusCode, payload, response, durationMs and createdAt (UTC). Use limit (1–50) and event to filter. 404 if the webhook isn't visible to the calling key; honours the same number scope as GET /api/v1/webhooks.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Webhook id.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of events to return (default 50).

parameters.name: event
parameters.description: Filter by event name (e.g. message.delivered).

parameters.name: x-whatsapp-number-id
parameters.description: Scopes to a single number (required for per-number OAuth grants).

get.description: Returns a remote pairing token's data (validity, number, name). Token-public endpoint — used by the hosted /connect/<token> page; no API key required. 404 if invalid/expired.

parameters.name: token
parameters.description: Remote pairing token.

post.description: Generates the QR code + pairing code for the number tied to the token and marks the instance CONNECTING. Token-public endpoint (the /connect/<token> page); no API key required. 409 if already OPEN. Real side effect: starts the connection.

parameters.name: token
parameters.description: Remote pairing token.

get.description: Syncs and returns the token number's connection state (poll until OPEN). Once connected, the single-use token is cleared. Token-public endpoint; no API key required.

parameters.name: token
parameters.description: Remote pairing token.

get.description:
Returns the connect page's (/connect/<token>) branding/white-label: logo, colors, title/subtitle, support link and the white-label toggle. Requires a tenant-scoped API key — number-scoped keys get 403 (NUMBER_SCOPE_NOT_ALLOWED).

**Requires a tenant-scoped key.**

403.description: Number-scoped key used on a tenant-only endpoint

example.error: Number-scoped keys cannot call tenant endpoints

put.description:
Upserts the tenant branding. All fields optional: send null to clear, omit to keep. Colors are hex #RRGGBB; URLs https. Requires a tenant-scoped API key (403 NUMBER_SCOPE_NOT_ALLOWED for number-scoped keys). Logo upload: POST /api/branding/logo (dashboard session, multipart PNG/JPG/WebP ≤2MB).

**Requires a tenant-scoped key.**

403.description: Number-scoped key used on a tenant-only endpoint

example.error: Number-scoped keys cannot call tenant endpoints

get.description: Returns the resolved branding for a connect page (precedence: per-link override > tenant branding > Pilot Status default). Accepts both token shapes (remote-pairing UUID or Meta Embedded Signup JWT). Token-public endpoint; always 200 (falls back to defaults for an invalid token).

parameters.name: token
parameters.description: Connect page token (remote-pairing UUID or Meta JWT).

post.description: Completes a hosted Embedded Signup: the token (minted by POST /api/v1/numbers/remote-pairing { provider: META }) carries the tenant, so the browser flow needs no ps_ key. Called by the /connect/<token> page once Facebook returns the chosen phoneNumberId/wabaId/code. Real side effect: creates the Meta number. 401 INVALID_SESSION if the token expires.

parameters.name: token
parameters.description: Embedded Signup session token.

post.description: Creates a short-lived embed session token (JWT) to mount the chat (surface:"chat") or connect (surface:"connect") microfrontend on your own site via the @pilot-status/embed SDK. allowedOrigins (1–20 exact origins) restricts where the iframe may run; brandingOverride applies per-session branding; ttlSeconds controls validity (chat 15min, connect 30min, max 60min). Works with a number- or tenant-scoped key — a number-scoped key can only embed its own number. Refresh via POST /api/public/embed/refresh.

whatsappNumberIds.description: Numbers to expose in chat (required for a tenant key on surface:chat).

allowedOrigins.description: Exact origins (scheme://host[:port]) where the iframe may run.

brandingOverride.description: Per-session branding (logo/colors/title) overriding the tenant branding.

ttlSeconds.description: Token validity in seconds (default 900 chat / 1800 connect).

get.description:
Lists the bound number's calls, newest first. Query: limit (1–100, default 25) and before (ISO 8601 cursor — only calls created strictly before that instant). DTOs never carry SDPs here. Meta Cloud API and web (unofficial) numbers (other providers → 400 FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED).

**Requires a number-scoped key.**

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Page size (default 25).

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: Cursor: only calls created strictly before this instant.

403.description: Tenant-scoped key used on a number-scoped endpoint

example.error: Tenant-scoped keys cannot call number endpoints

post.description:
Starts a business-initiated voice call to the user. Body: { to, sdp?, sdpType?:"offer", bizOpaqueCallbackData? }. Meta numbers: sdp is REQUIRED — the RFC 8866 offer from YOUR WebRTC client (business side must use a=setup:active) — and a prior user permission is required (otherwise Meta returns 138006 → META_CALL_PERMISSION_REQUIRED); billed per minute by Meta, directly on your WABA, only when answered. Web (unofficial) numbers: OMIT sdp — media is handled server-side — no permission step and no Meta billing.

**Requires a number-scoped key.**

sdp.description: SDP offer from your WebRTC client (a=setup:active). Required on Meta numbers; omit on web numbers.

bizOpaqueCallbackData.description: Opaque data echoed back on the terminate event.

403.description: Tenant-scoped key used on a number-scoped endpoint

example.error: Tenant-scoped keys cannot call number endpoints

get.description:
Returns one call of the key's number. {callId} accepts the Pilot Status id (call_...) OR the provider call id (Meta wacid / Evolution GO CallID). SDPs are omitted by default — pass ?includeSdp=1 to receive sdpOffer/sdpAnswer (needed e.g. to answer an inbound call on a Meta number; always null on web numbers). 404 CALL_NOT_FOUND when the call does not belong to the number. Meta and web numbers.

**Requires a number-scoped key.**

parameters.name: callId
parameters.description: Pilot Status id (call_...) or provider call id (wacid / CallID).

parameters.name: includeSdp
parameters.description: Include sdpOffer/sdpAnswer in the DTO.

403.description: Tenant-scoped key used on a number-scoped endpoint

example.error: Tenant-scoped keys cannot call number endpoints

post.description:
Sends the SDP answer early (before accept) to reduce audio clipping at call start. OPTIONAL — the call only connects on accept. Body: { sdp, sdpType?:"answer" }. {callId} accepts id or wacid. Meta numbers only (a signaling concept; web numbers → 400 FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED).

**Requires a number-scoped key.**

parameters.name: callId
parameters.description: Pilot Status id (call_...) or Meta wacid.

sdp.description: SDP answer from your WebRTC client.

403.description: Tenant-scoped key used on a number-scoped endpoint

example.error: Tenant-scoped keys cannot call number endpoints

post.description:
Answers a user-initiated call (UIC). Meta numbers: body { sdp, sdpType?:"answer" } — the business-side SDP answer; audio flows browser↔WhatsApp directly. Web numbers: no body — media is handled server-side (use /play and /realtime-session). {callId} accepts id or the provider call id. Persists status ACCEPTED.

**Requires a number-scoped key.**

parameters.name: callId
parameters.description: Pilot Status id (call_...) or provider call id (wacid / CallID).

sdp.description: SDP answer from your WebRTC client. Required on Meta numbers; omit on web numbers.

403.description: Tenant-scoped key used on a number-scoped endpoint

example.error: Tenant-scoped keys cannot call number endpoints

post.description:
Declines an incoming call (no body). {callId} accepts id or the provider call id. Persists status REJECTED. Meta and web numbers.

**Requires a number-scoped key.**

parameters.name: callId
parameters.description: Pilot Status id (call_...) or provider call id (wacid / CallID).

403.description: Tenant-scoped key used on a number-scoped endpoint

example.error: Tenant-scoped keys cannot call number endpoints

post.description:
Hangs up an active call (no body). {callId} accepts id or the provider call id. Persists status COMPLETED; the call.ended webhook carries the duration when the call was answered. Meta and web numbers.

**Requires a number-scoped key.**

parameters.name: callId
parameters.description: Pilot Status id (call_...) or provider call id (wacid / CallID).

403.description: Tenant-scoped key used on a number-scoped endpoint

example.error: Tenant-scoped keys cannot call number endpoints

post.description:
Plays an audio file into an active call. Body: { mediaUrl } — a public http(s) URL of an .mp3/.wav/.opus file (media travels by URL, like every media send on web numbers). When the call is not active yet the audio is queued and starts as soon as it connects. Web (unofficial) numbers ONLY — Meta numbers return 400 FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED (their media flows via WebRTC).

**Requires a number-scoped key.**

parameters.name: callId
parameters.description: Pilot Status id (call_...) or provider call id (CallID).

mediaUrl.description: Public URL of the audio to play (.mp3, .wav or .opus).

403.description: Tenant-scoped key used on a number-scoped endpoint

example.error: Tenant-scoped keys cannot call number endpoints

post.description:
Opens a short-lived full-duplex media session on an active call. Body: { mode: "talk" | "listen" } — talk sends YOUR audio and receives the peer's; listen only receives. Returns { wsUrl, token, expiresInSeconds }: connect a WebSocket to wsUrl and exchange RAW binary PCM16 little-endian audio frames (plain WebSocket transport, NOT WebRTC; single-use token, ~2 min). Web numbers ONLY — Meta → 400 FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED.

**Requires a number-scoped key.**

parameters.name: callId
parameters.description: Pilot Status id (call_...) or provider call id (CallID).

mode.description: talk = send and receive audio; listen = receive only.

403.description: Tenant-scoped key used on a number-scoped endpoint

example.error: Tenant-scoped keys cannot call number endpoints

get.description:
Returns the number's calling object (status, call_icon_visibility, callback_permission_status, call_hours, ...) exactly as Meta exposes it. SIP credentials are never requested nor returned.

**Requires a number-scoped key.**

403.description: Tenant-scoped key used on a number-scoped endpoint

example.error: Tenant-scoped keys cannot call number endpoints

put.description:
PARTIAL update of the calling object: status, call_icon_visibility, callback_permission_status, call_hours, and voicemail (sip is rejected). Note: call_hours REPLACES the stored object entirely (Meta semantics). Numbers with a messaging tier < 2,000/day cannot enable it (Meta 138015).

**Requires a number-scoped key.**

call_icon_visibility.description: Visibility of the call icon in the user's WhatsApp.

callback_permission_status.description: Lets the user ask the business to call back.

call_hours.description: Calling hours window (REPLACES the whole object).

403.description: Tenant-scoped key used on a number-scoped endpoint

example.error: Tenant-scoped keys cannot call number endpoints

get.description:
Probes whether the business may call ?to=<phone>: permission status (no_permission | temporary | permanent, with expiration_time) plus per-action limits (send_call_permission_request / start_call). Required before a BIC.

**Requires a number-scoped key.**

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: User phone to probe.

403.description: Tenant-scoped key used on a number-scoped endpoint

example.error: Tenant-scoped keys cannot call number endpoints

post.description:
Sends the interactive call_permission_request message to the user (valid inside the 24h service window). Body: { to, text? }. The user's answer arrives as the call.permission_updated webhook event. Meta limits apply (138009 → META_CALL_PERMISSION_REQUEST_LIMIT).

**Requires a number-scoped key.**

text.example: Podemos te ligar para finalizar seu pedido?

403.description: Tenant-scoped key used on a number-scoped endpoint

example.error: Tenant-scoped keys cannot call number endpoints

get.description: Returns the Chatwoot integration status for the WhatsApp number. The number comes from the key scope (number key) or the x-whatsapp-number-id header (tenant key / OAuth). The Chatwoot access token is NEVER returned (only hasToken).

parameters.name: x-whatsapp-number-id
parameters.description: Scopes to a single number (required for tenant keys / per-number OAuth grants).

400.description: No number resolvable (tenant key / per-number grant without x-whatsapp-number-id)

404.description: The number does not belong to this tenant

post.description: Connects the WhatsApp number to a Chatwoot account: validates the credentials against the instance, creates the inbox and starts mirroring. Tip: dry-run with POST /v1/chatwoot/test first. The access token is NEVER returned.

parameters.name: x-whatsapp-number-id
parameters.description: Scopes to a single number (required for tenant keys / per-number OAuth grants).

instanceUrl.description: Base URL of the Chatwoot instance.

channelMode.description: Channel preference: auto (default), api (force mirror inbox), native (force whatsapp_cloud inbox when eligible).

400.description: Validation error, or Chatwoot rejected the credentials

404.description: The number does not belong to this tenant

patch.description: Updates an existing Chatwoot integration (404 NOT_CONNECTED otherwise). enabled toggles mirroring; rotating any credential re-runs the full connect and recreates the inbox; action="reconnect" rebuilds the inbox reusing the saved credentials; channelMode switches the inbox mode.

parameters.name: x-whatsapp-number-id
parameters.description: Scopes to a single number (required for tenant keys / per-number OAuth grants).

enabled.description: false pauses mirroring without disconnecting; true resumes.

instanceUrl.description: Base URL of the Chatwoot instance.

action.description: reconnect = rebuild the inbox reusing the saved credentials.

channelMode.description: Channel preference: auto (default), api (force mirror inbox), native (force whatsapp_cloud inbox when eligible).

400.description: Validation error, or Chatwoot rejected the credentials

404.description: Chatwoot is not connected for this number

example.error: Chatwoot is not connected for this number

delete.description: Removes the integration and the native provider webhook (idempotent). Pass deleteInbox=true to ALSO delete the remote Chatwoot inbox.

parameters.name: x-whatsapp-number-id
parameters.description: Scopes to a single number (required for tenant keys / per-number OAuth grants).

parameters.name: deleteInbox
parameters.description: true = also delete the remote Chatwoot inbox (default false).

404.description: The number does not belong to this tenant

post.description: Dry-run of Chatwoot credentials WITHOUT creating an inbox or writing anything. Omit userAccessToken to reuse the saved token. Returns { ok, checks: [...] }.

parameters.name: x-whatsapp-number-id
parameters.description: Scopes to a single number (required for tenant keys / per-number OAuth grants).

instanceUrl.description: Base URL of the Chatwoot instance.

userAccessToken.description: Chatwoot access token; omit to reuse the saved token.

400.description: Validation error, or Chatwoot rejected the credentials

404.description: The number does not belong to this tenant

get.description: Published Meta / WhatsApp Business Platform per-message rate for a single market + currency + category. Global reference data - NOT the tenant's real billing. Call with no params for metadata (available markets, currencies, categories). Provide market + currency + category for the flat price; add tiers=true for volume tiers. Any valid API key works.

parameters.name: market
parameters.description: Market code. Codes: AR Argentina, BR Brazil, CL Chile, CO Colombia, EG Egypt, FR France, DE Germany, HK Hong Kong, HU Hungary, IN India, ID Indonesia, IL Israel, IT Italy, MY Malaysia, MX Mexico, NL Netherlands, NG Nigeria, NAM North America, PK Pakistan, PE Peru, PL Poland, QA Qatar, RO Romania, RU Russia, SA Saudi Arabia, SG Singapore, ZA South Africa, ES Spain, TR Turkey, AE United Arab Emirates, GB United Kingdom, GLO Other, AFR Rest of Africa, APAC Rest of Asia Pacific, CEEU Rest of Central & Eastern Europe, LATAM Rest of Latin America, MDE Rest of Middle East, WEU Rest of Western Europe.

parameters.name: currency
parameters.description: ISO 4217 currency.

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Message category (required together with market + currency).

parameters.name: tiers
parameters.description: Include the volume tiers (cheaper price at higher monthly volume) in the response.

200.description: The flat price for the market+currency+category, or metadata when called with no params.

pricePerMessage.description: List price per delivered message in the requested currency. 0 = free (Service).

volumeTiers.description: Only present with tiers=true. Cheaper price at higher monthly volume.

404.description: PRICE_NOT_FOUND - no published rate for that market+currency+category.
